exercises.name: Standing Banded Scapular Retraction [Strength Rebels]
exercises.description: Standing Banded Scapular Retraction [Strength Rebels]

exercises.name: Standing Bent Over Medicine Ball Slams
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball in front of your chest. 

Hinge at your hips into a bent-over position with a flat back and slight knee bend. Explosively drive the ball downward toward the floor, engaging your core and lats as you slam it with power. 

Catch or pick up the ball on the rebound, return to the bent-over position, and immediately repeat the movement in a continuous, forceful rhythm.

exercises.name: Standing cable rope OH Tricep ext
Standing Calf Raise Pulses
exercises.description:
Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart and your core engaged. Lift your heels off the floor so you’re balanced on the balls of your feet. 

From this raised position, perform small controlled pulses up and down without letting your heels touch the ground. 

Keep your legs straight but not locked and maintain steady balance throughout the movement. Focus on squeezing your calves at the top of each pulse.

exercises.name: Standing Cross Body Glute Stretch
exercises.description:
1) Stand on one leg. 

2) Pull your knee across your body and upward towards the opposite shoulder. 

3) Hold. 

4) Then repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Standing Figure-4 to Reverse Lunge
exercises.description: Stand tall, cross one ankle over the opposite knee into a figure-4 position, then step back into a reverse lunge.

exercises.name: Standing Hamstring Curl
exercises.description: Stand with cable attached to one ankle. Curl the leg behind you, bringing heel toward glute. Keep hips steady.

exercises.name: Standing Oblique Crunch
exercises.description:
Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart and hands placed behind your head or lightly touching your temples.

 Engage your core and bend your torso sideways, bringing your elbow toward the same-side hip while keeping your hips stable.

exercises.name: Standing Piriformis Stretch
exercises.alternateName: Piriformis Stretch on Bench Glute figure four stretch
exercises.description:
1. When you're doing this stretch, rest your leg just above the knee not on the knee itself.

2. Sit back and try to open up your hip. Make sure you have something to hold on to for balance.

3. Hold this position for 10 - 30 seconds.

4. Once you're completed one side, do the other.

exercises.name: Standing Shoulder Flexion
exercises.description:
1. Start standing with your hands down by your sides, and thumbs pointing upwards.

2. While keeping your collar bone in place, lift your arm up towards the ceiling, pointing your thumb upwards. 

3. Slowly lower back down to the start position. 

4. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Standing Straight Bar Cable Curl - The Proper Lift - BPI Sports
exercises.description: Standing Straight Bar Cable Curl - The Proper Lift - BPI Sports

exercises.name: Star Side Full Plank
exercises.description: From a side plank, lift your top leg and arm into a star position. Keep your core engaged and body aligned.

exercises.name: Static Distal Hamstring Stretch
exercises.description:
1) Place your heel on an elevated ledge or bench. 

2) Slightly bend the knee to anchor the distal hamstring, but try not to over activate your hamstring (back of thigh). 

3) Tip your pelvis forward, lengthening the hamstring. 

4) Hold. 

5) Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Static Forearm Stretch
exercises.description:
1) Maintain your upper arm in a static position with elbow locked out. 

2) Let your wrist drop backward, palm up and away form the body. 

3) Apply light pressure in the direction that the wrist has dropped forward. Then bring the arm in an outstretched position beside the body.

4) Hold. 

5) Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Static Frog Stretch
exercises.alternateName: Frog Stretch Balasana Variant, adductor stretch
exercises.description:
1. When performing this stretch , you'll want to ease into it to ensure that you don’t pull anything.

2. Sit back and come down to your elbows and rest to this position.

exercises.name: Static Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.description:
1. When performing this stretch, you'll want to ensure that you keep your abdominal on and activate your glutes to tack your knees underneath you. That will set the hip flexor to be anchored and from there, lean forward and feel a deeper stretch.

2. Kneel on a mat and bring your right knee up so the bottom of your foot is on the floor and extend your left leg out behind you so the top of your foot is on the floor.

3. Shift your weight forward until you feel a stretch in your hip. Hold for 15 seconds.

4. Once you're done one side, repeat it on the other.

exercises.name: Static Lat and Tricep Stretch
exercises.description:
1) Extend a bent arm overhead.

2) With elbow pointing straight up in the air apply a medial pull towards the opposite side of your body with your opposing hand.

3) Hold. 

4) Repeat on the other arm.

exercises.name: Static Lateral Neck Stretch
exercises.description:
1) Allow your head to tip sideways towards your shoulder. Don't let it turn.

2) Lightly rest your hand from the side of your body you tilted your head towards on your head. 

3) Hold. 

4) Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Static Neck Extension Stretch
exercises.description:
1) Allow your head to extend and fall backward.

2) Place your fingers on your chin and apply light pressure. 

3) Hold.

exercises.name: Static Neck Flexion Stretch
exercises.description:
1) Allow the head to fall forward towards your chest.

2) Rest your hands lightly on the back of your head. 

3) Hold.

exercises.name: Static Oblique and Lat Stretch
exercises.description:
1) While standing cross your legs. Keep both legs straight.

2) Reach with your arm and torso upward on the side with the leg crossed in front.  

3) Side bend from foot to hand laterally, without twisting. 

4) Hold without bouncing.

5) Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Static Overhead Reach with Thoracic Extension
exercises.description:
1) Extend your arms directly overhead, biceps to ears. 

2) Open the front part of your rib cage to allow your thoracic spine to go into extension. Keep your abdominals on to prevent excessive lumbar extension.

3) Hold.

exercises.name: Static Pigeon Stretch
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, ensure to lower yourself down into position as to not strain anything. 

2. Get down on the floor, roll your right leg underneath you, stretch your left leg back. 

3. Use your elbows to support the upper body. Hold.

4. When you've completed one side, do the other.

exercises.name: Static Proximal Hamstring Stretch
exercises.description:
1) Place your heel on an elevated ledge. 

2) Keep your knee straight by allowing gravity to let it drop into alignment with your hip and foot. 

3) Tilt your pelvis forward until you feel your hamstring (the back of your thigh) lightly get taunt/stretched. 

4) Hold. 

5) Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Static Quadricep Stretch
exercises.description:
1) Have one hand placed on something for stability if possible. 

2) With the other hand, grab your foot and bring your hip, knee, and foot all in line with one another vertically (perpendicular to the ground). 

3) Hold. 

4) Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Static Rear Delt Stretch
exercises.description:
1. Start standing with one arm fully extended, across your body. 

2. Gently draw the arm in towards your body until you feel a light stretch in the back of your shoulder. 

3. Breathe and hold this stretch position.

4. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Static Rear Foot Elevated Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.description:
1. When you're doing this mobility exercise, you'll want to ease into the back foot slowly as to not over stretched of have the muscles contract.

2. Kneel on a mat and bring your right knee up so the bottom of your foot is on the floor and extend your left leg out behind you so the top of your foot is on the bench.

3. Shift your weight forward until you feel a stretch in your hip. Hold for 15 seconds.

4. Once you're done one side, repeat it on the other.

exercises.name: Static Side Lunge Stretch
exercises.description:
1.Assume an athletic standing position, with the knees and hips slightly bent, feet shoulder-width apart and head up. This will be your starting position.

2.Staying low, take a slow, lateral step to the right. Keep your toes pointed forward. 

3.Extend the left knee, driving your weight to the right, flexing the knee and hip into a side lunge. 

4.Maintain good posture through the entire spine, keeping your head up.

5.Pause at the bottom of the motion, and then extend through the working leg to return to a standing position, transitioning into a lunge to the opposite side.

exercises.name: Static Wrist Flexion Stretch
exercises.description:
1) Maintain your upper arm in a static position.

2) Let your wrist drop forward, palm down and towards the body. 

3) Apply light pressure in the direction that the wrist has dropped forward.

4) Hold. 

5) Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Station 2 - F45Playoffs - Bench Hops
exercises.description: Station 2 - F45Playoffs - Bench Hops

exercises.name: Step-Up to Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
Stand facing a bench or elevated platform. Step one foot onto the bench, pushing through the heel to lift your body upward, and immediately drive your opposite knee toward your chest at the top. 

Step back down to the floor, then perform a reverse lunge.

exercises.name: Straddle Side Stretch Alternating
exercises.description: Sit with legs wide. Alternate reaching one hand to the opposite foot while extending the other arm overhead.

exercises.name: Straddle Stretch
exercises.description: Sit on the floor with legs extended wide. Lean forward, reaching your hands toward the floor. Hold and stretch.

exercises.name: Straight arm pull down with shoulder retraction
